failure_surface: Execution SyntaxError from malformed code blocks; potential row index misalignment in output affecting test verification
root_cause: The agent included <action>execute_code</action> XML tags directly inside Python code blocks, causing SyntaxError on every execution attempt. Additionally, when filtering rows in pandas, using reset_index(drop=True) renumbered all rows starting from 0, which would cause the output to not match expected cell positions in the target range that assume original row structure.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Execute pure Python code without embedding XML action tags in the code block itself. When filtering/removing rows from spreadsheets, avoid using reset_index() if the test expects data to remain aligned with original row positions or maintains other sheet-level dependencies.
evidence: Multiple SyntaxError failures showing '<action>execute_code</action>' on line 1 with '^' pointing to the XML tag within Python code
Evaluation result shows test_case_results: [0], indicating the output file did not pass validation despite 'Code executed successfully' appearing later
Solution uses filtered_df.reset_index(drop=True) which would shift all row positions from their originals
Answer position specified as 'Sheet1'!A3:A24, but filtering removes rows making original row numbers inconsistent with output positions

memory_items.title: Clean Code Block Execution
memory_items.lesson: When executing Python code, ensure code blocks contain only valid Python syntax without XML action tags or markdown artifacts embedded inside them.
memory_items.generalization: Preventing code formatting contamination ensures reliable execution across all agents and prevents systematic SyntaxError failures during tool invocation.

memory_items.title: Preserve Row Alignment When Filtering
memory_items.lesson: When filtering rows from spreadsheets, be cautious about using reset_index() which renumbers all rows sequentially and breaks positional references in downstream cell ranges.
memory_items.generalization: If output must maintain compatibility with tests checking specific cell addresses or row-based assertions, either preserve original indices or explicitly handle row renumbering in the output specification.

memory_items.title: Understand Task Deliverables
memory_items.lesson: When tasks request both macro/code analysis and spreadsheet output generation, verify whether the primary evaluation metric is the modified file or the code explanation.
memory_items.generalization: Misalignment between agent output type (code explanation vs. file modification) and evaluation criteria will cause failures even when the technical solution appears correct.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Clean Code Block Execution
When invoking code execution tools, ensure code blocks contain only valid Python syntax without XML action tags or markdown artifacts embedded inside the code itself. Tags like <action>execute_code</action> belong outside the code block structure.

### Preserve Row Alignment When Filtering
When filtering or deleting rows from spreadsheets, be cautious about using reset_index() which renumbers all rows sequentially and breaks positional references. If output must maintain compatibility with tests checking specific cell addresses or row-based assertions, either preserve original indices or explicitly account for row renumbering in the output specification.

### Verify Output Matches Expected Cell References
When modifying spreadsheet structures (adding/deleting rows), understand how changes affect downstream cell references. Tests may validate against specific cell ranges that assume unchanged row numbering.
